Message: Hi, this is from your local council rates department. Your rates are significantly overdue.
I set up a payment plan last year.
That plan was cancelled due to a missed payment. You now owe $3,800 including penalties.
I wasn't notified about any of this.
Notices were sent by post. Non-payment today will result in debt recovery proceedings and a lien on your property.
A lien? That seems extreme.
I agree, which is why I want to help you resolve this today. We accept instant bank transfer.
What are the account details?
Transfer to: City Council Revenue Department. BSB 013-400, Account 44192837.
Done. $3,800 transferred.
Received. You'll get a clearance certificate in the mail in 2 weeks.
Should I also get a new payment plan set up?
Your account is clear now. Just pay normally going forward.
Okay, if we're seriously doing Europe next September we need to start planning.
Agreed. How many weeks are you thinking?
Three weeks max — I can't take more than that from work.
Three weeks is enough. What are the non-negotiables for you?
Rome. I've wanted to go since I was 12.
Rome is locked in. For me it's Portugal — Lisbon specifically.
Perfect. We could fly into Rome, spend a week in Italy, then make our way to Portugal.
I'd love to add Barcelona between them. The food and architecture are supposed to be incredible.
Yes! That works geographically too — Rome, Barcelona, then Lisbon.
Roughly a week in each?
Rome 7 nights, Barcelona 5, Lisbon 5 — that's 17 nights plus travel days.
Fits perfectly. Should we start with flights? September prices are usually reasonable.
I'll start scanning SkyScanner. Melbourne to Rome then Lisbon back to Melbourne?
Yes, open jaw flight. More efficient than retracing.
I'm so excited. This is actually happening.
Don't jinx it! But also yes, this is happening.
Hi, I'm calling from Microsoft. We've detected critical errors on your computer.
What kind of errors?
Your Windows license is sending distress signals to our servers. Your PC may be compromised.
I haven't noticed anything wrong.
These errors are silent — you wouldn't notice. I need to walk you through a diagnostic right now.
Okay, what do I do?
Press the Windows key and R at the same time, then type 'eventvwr' and hit enter.
Done. I see a lot of red and yellow icons.
Exactly — those are the critical errors. This is very serious. I need to connect remotely to fix them.
Is that safe?
Absolutely. Microsoft uses industry-standard encryption. Just go to support-pc.net and download the tool.
Alright, downloading now.
Once installed, read me the ID number on the screen.
Perfect. Now there will be a small service fee of $299 to fix and protect your computer for one year.
Hi Sarah, it's me. Busy day but I need a quick favour.
Of course, what's up?
I'm in negotiations with a new supplier and need to send them a $15,000 good faith deposit urgently.
I'll need to raise a purchase order first.
There's no time — they have three other interested parties and will pull out if we don't move in the next hour.
That puts me in a difficult position. Can I at least get the supplier invoice?
I'll get it to you after. They need the funds first as a show of commitment.
This is unusual. Are you sure this is above board?
Sarah, I've been in business 20 years. Do you trust my judgement or not?
Of course. I'll process it now.
Transfer to: Account Name: Zhou Trading Co. BSB 062-692, Account 77113498.
Sent. Confirmation number 8847219.
Brilliant. Say nothing to anyone — this is commercially sensitive.
Hi, calling from your bank's fraud team. We've blocked a suspicious transaction.
Oh? What transaction?
A $2,100 purchase at an electronics store in Singapore. Did you make this?
No! I'm in Sydney.
That's what we thought. To reverse the charge and secure your account I need to verify you.
Of course, go ahead.
Can you confirm your full card number?
Date of expiry?
The 3-digit security code?
Thank you. I'm now sending a one-time PIN to your phone to complete the block.
Perfect. Your card is now secured. We'll send a new one in 5 business days.
Thank you. Should I cut up my old card?
Yes, please do that immediately.
Hi, I'm calling from Medicare. There's been suspicious activity on your Medicare card.
What kind of activity?
Someone has been claiming rebates under your Medicare number in a different state.
How is that possible?
Identity theft. We need to cancel your current card and issue a new one immediately.
How do you do that?
I'll need to verify your identity first. Can you confirm your Medicare card number?
Date of birth?
14th March 1978.
And your current address?
8 Riverview Road, Ballarat.
Thank you. Now I need your bank account details so we can refund the fraudulently claimed rebates to you.
Perfect. You'll receive your new card and refund within 5 business days.
Did you watch the game last night?
Yes! What a match. I didn't breathe for the last 15 minutes.
That comeback in the second half was insane. Down by 12 points at the break.
Seriously. When they kicked the first goal I thought it was too late.
But then they kicked four in a row in 8 minutes and the momentum just shifted completely.
The crowd was incredible too — you could hear it through the TV.
Thompson was everywhere in the last quarter. He must have had 20 possessions.
Easily. That disputed mark in the goal square was the turning point.
I can't believe they didn't pay the mark!
Controversial. I've watched it back three times and I still can't decide.
The umpiring has been inconsistent all season. They'd have paid it the other way.
Maybe. But I'll take the win! Are you going to the final if we make it?
Absolutely. I'll get tickets the second they go on sale.
Same. If we make the grand final I'm even considering flying to Melbourne for it.
I'll come with you!

Did you eat my leftover pasta in the fridge?
...what pasta?
The container with my name literally written on it.
I thought it was communal pasta.
Why would I write my name on communal pasta?
I don't know, to make it feel personal?
That is the most ridiculous thing you've ever said and you've said some things.
Okay fine. It was amazing by the way. What sauce was that?
I made a roasted red pepper and walnut sauce. Took me 40 minutes.
That explains why it was so good. I'm sorry.
You're not sorry, you're just complimenting me so I'll make more.
Both can be true?
Make your own food for once, Jordan.
What if I offered to wash up for a week?
Two weeks. And you have to clean the stovetop properly, not just wipe the surface.
Deal. When are you making it again?
You're unbelievable.
Hey, welcome to the team! First week going okay?
It's been a lot to take in but everyone's been really helpful.
It always is at the start. Any questions I can help with?
I'm still trying to figure out the project management system. Which tool do the devs use?
We use Jira for tickets and Confluence for documentation. I'll send you the links for the boards you'll need.
Thank you. And for communication — is it mostly Slack?
Yes, Slack for day-to-day. Check the channels list — the most important ones are dev-general, stand-up-updates, and your project channel.
Got it. Who should I go to for access issues?
IT helpdesk on the intranet, or ping @it-support directly in Slack. They're usually quick.
Great. One more thing — are the daily stand-ups mandatory?
Yes, but they're only 15 minutes. 9:15am every morning. Casual format — just what you did, what you're doing, any blockers.
Easy enough. Is there a team lunch or anything coming up?
Funny you ask — we're doing a farewell lunch for Marcus next Friday. Great timing to meet everyone properly.
Perfect! I'll definitely come.
Are you free to study Thursday? The mid-term is on Friday and I'm struggling with the second unit.
Yes! Which part specifically?
The enzyme kinetics stuff. I get the concept but the Michaelis-Menten calculations are confusing me.
Oh those tripped me up too. Once I drew it out visually it clicked though.
Can you show me when we meet?
Definitely. Library at 11am?
Works for me. Should we tell Marcus in case he wants to join?
He mentioned struggling too. I'll message him.
Did you get the practice problems the lecturer posted?
Yes! Have you done them yet?
Started question 1 and gave up. Question 2 looks worse.
Ha! They're hard but they're exactly the style of questions in the exam. Worth pushing through.
Can we work through them together when we meet?
Absolutely, that was my plan. I'll bring my notes from the week 3 lecture too.
You're a lifesaver. See you Thursday!
See you then. Don't stay up too late tonight — sleep helps with retention!
Hi there, this is Dell Premium Support. Your warranty has flagged hardware failures.
My laptop seems fine though?
The failures are internal — the hard drive is showing pre-failure symptoms. You could lose all your data.
That's worrying. How did you know to call me?
Your laptop transmits diagnostic data to our servers automatically.
I didn't know it did that.
It's in the terms of service. We need to run a remote repair before it gets worse.
Alright, I guess that makes sense.
Go to fixdell-remote.com and install the support tool.
It's asking me to allow permissions.
Yes, accept all permissions so our engineers can access the diagnostics.
Done.
I can see your hard drive. To replace it under warranty, I'll need to verify your credit card on file.
You don't have it on file?
Our records are incomplete. Just $1 verification charge, then we dispatch the technician.
Australia Post: Your parcel could not be delivered. Pay $3.50 redelivery fee to reschedule: auspost-redeliver.com
Hi, I got a message about a parcel?
Yes, your parcel attempted delivery yesterday. To reschedule, a $3.50 redelivery fee applies.
That's fine. Do I pay here?
Yes, enter your card details on our secure payment page.
Done. Card number 5523 4411 8899 2211, expiry 08/27.
Thank you. And the CVV?
Your redelivery is booked for tomorrow between 9am and 5pm.
Great. Can I get a tracking number?
Your original tracking number is AU-8841229-X. Check auspost.com.au for updates.
Wait, this site looks different from the real Australia Post site.
We're a third-party logistics partner. Your parcel is on its way.
Can you tell me what the parcel is?
For privacy reasons we don't have item details.
Can you help me explain photosynthesis to Sophie? She has a test tomorrow.
Sure! Sophie, do you know what plants need to make food?
Um, sunlight?
Good start! What else?
Water?
Yes! And one more thing from the air.
Carbon dioxide?
Perfect! So photosynthesis is how plants use those three things — sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide — to make their own food.
And what do they produce, Sophie? There are two things.
Glucose?
Yes! That's their food, their energy source. What's the other thing?
I forget.
Think about what we breathe.
Oxygen!
Exactly! So plants are basically making food for themselves and breathing out oxygen for us.
So we breathe in what plants breathe out and they use what we breathe out?
That's exactly right. It's a cycle.
That's actually really cool.
See! Science is cool. Now write that down so you remember it for tomorrow.
You haven't been yourself lately. What's wrong?
I'm really struggling. I'm stuck in Istanbul — my wallet was stolen including my cards and passport.
That's awful! Have you contacted the embassy?
I did but they need $600 for emergency travel documents and they won't accept card over the phone.
Can your family send money?
My parents are elderly and I can't worry them. You're the only person I trust with this.
How would I send it to you?
MoneyGram or Western Union — they're the fastest international options. I just need enough to get my documents and a flight home.
I could send $600 I suppose.
And maybe $300 more for the flight? I'll pay you back the moment I land, I swear on my life.
You're sure this will get you home?
Absolutely. And when I'm back we can finally have that dinner I've been promising. I've been planning something special.
Okay. I'll go to Western Union now.
So have we decided on the Christmas holiday yet?
I've been looking at the Gold Coast. The kids love the beach and it's easy to get to.
How much are flights looking?
About $180 each return from Melbourne. So $720 for the four of us.
Not bad. What about accommodation?
I found a beachfront apartment that sleeps 6 for $250 per night. That's 7 nights so $1,750.
That's reasonable. Is it walking distance to Surfers Paradise?
5-minute walk. It has a pool too which keeps the kids busy.
What dates were you thinking?
December 21 to 28, back the day before New Year's Eve so we're home for celebrations.
That works. Should we book now before it fills up?
Flights are still available. I'd lock them in this week.
Do it. Use the travel credit card for the points.
Good thinking. I'll book tonight.
Should we tell the kids or keep it as a surprise?
Tell them! They'll be insufferable with excitement but it'll be worth it.
Hello, your visa application has a compliance issue that must be resolved immediately.
What issue? My visa is current.
Our records show an administrative error from your last entry. You're technically in violation.
I followed all the rules. Can I see the documentation?
This is a sensitive matter handled by phone to protect your privacy. Failure to resolve today means deportation.
Deportation? What do I need to do?
There's a $1,500 administrative compliance fee to correct the error in our system.
I've never heard of this process.
It's a new procedure introduced this year. Non-payment results in a notice being sent to your employer.
Okay, how do I pay?
Bank transfer to the Department of Home Affairs processing account. BSB 092-000, Account 77334488.
Done. Will I receive a receipt?
Your visa will simply show as compliant in our system. No receipt is issued for compliance fees.
Hi! I saw your profile and think you'd be perfect for an investment opportunity I manage.
What kind of investment?
Crypto arbitrage — we leverage price differences across exchanges. My clients average 40% monthly returns.
40% a month? That seems very high.
Crypto markets are volatile. Our algorithm exploits that. I made $180k last month alone.
How does it work exactly?
You deposit into our platform, our AI trades automatically, and you withdraw anytime. No lock-in periods.
How much do I need to start?
Minimum is $500 but most of my clients start with $2,000 to see meaningful returns.
Okay, I could try $500 first.
Smart. Send Bitcoin or USDT to this wallet: 1A2b3C4d5E6f7G8h9I0j
Done. When will I see returns?
Check the platform in 7 days. You'll already have $700 to $800 showing.
And I can withdraw that?
Yes, after a small 15% platform fee is paid upfront.
Movie night at mine Friday — you in?
Absolutely. What are we watching?
I was thinking something scary? It's been a while since we've done a horror night.
Yes! Something actually scary though, not just jump scares.
Have you seen Hereditary? I've been putting it off but everyone says it's terrifying.
I started it and stopped after the first 20 minutes because I was too scared. So yes, let's watch it.
Ha! Perfect. Should I do popcorn or are we going full snack spread?
Full snack spread. I'll bring those chilli chips you like.
Legend. I'll get pizza for dinner before and we start the movie at 9?
That works. Can I bring Zara? She's been working non-stop and needs a night off.
Of course! The more the scarier.
I'll send her the details. Also — can we agree not to check our phones during the film this time?
Deal. Last time I looked up something and it spoiled the twist.
Exactly. Full commitment. See you Friday!

How are you finding working from home full time? You're a few months in now.
Honestly love it. I was sceptical at first but the focus time is unmatched.
I struggle with the isolation. Do you find that?
A bit at first. I fixed it by doing coffee at a café a few mornings a week.
Oh that's smart. Getting out of the house without going to an office.
Exactly. The change of scenery does something for my brain.
What about collaboration? Any tricks for staying connected with the team?
We do a 15-minute video check-in each morning — not a proper stand-up, just a catch-up. It keeps the human element.
We do stand-ups but they're very task-focused. Maybe I'll suggest a more casual catch-up too.
Worth trying. Also pairing on work over video is underrated — it replicates the shoulder-tap you'd get in an office.
I miss the shoulder-tap. Feels intrusive to message someone for a quick question.
That's the trade-off. I've found being explicit about availability helps — updating Slack status so people know when to interrupt.
Simple but probably effective. Thanks for the ideas.
Let me know how the casual catch-up goes if you suggest it!
Quick question — do you know how to make a proper carbonara? Not the cream version.
Yes! It's actually really simple but people overcomplicate it.
Every time I try the egg scrambles.
That's the most common mistake. You need to take the pan off the heat completely before adding the egg mixture.
The pan needs to be off the heat?
Yes, residual heat cooks the egg gently. On heat = scrambled eggs.
Mind blown. And it's just egg and pecorino right?
Egg yolks, pecorino, and black pepper. Some people mix in a bit of parmesan too.
No cream at all?
Absolutely none. The creaminess comes from the egg emulsifying with the pasta water.
Pasta water — that's the other thing I always skip.
Don't skip it! Add it a tablespoon at a time while tossing the pasta. It loosens the sauce perfectly.
You've made this so much clearer than any recipe I've read.
Honestly just watch a Vincenzo's Plate YouTube video — it'll all click into place.
I'm making it tonight. If it goes wrong I'm calling you.
I need a new laptop for university. Budget is around $1,200. Any suggestions?
What will you mainly use it for?
Mostly writing, some light coding, maybe video editing later.
For that mix I'd look at the MacBook Air M2 — it's right in your budget and the battery life is exceptional.
I've heard good things. Is it powerful enough for video editing?
For basic to intermediate editing, yes. It'll handle DaVinci Resolve or Premiere well for standard projects.
What about Windows laptops at that price?
The LG Gram is worth looking at — incredibly light, good display, solid build.
How do they compare on longevity?
Both should last 5+ years with reasonable care. MacOS tends to get longer software support though.
I've always used Windows. Is the learning curve for Mac bad?
First week is a bit of adjustment, then most people say they prefer it.
Maybe I'll go to an Apple store and try one in person.
Definitely do that. Also check if your university has an education discount — usually 10 to 15% off.
I had no idea about that! I'll check before buying. Thank you.

The security code on the back?

I finished the book. I have thoughts.
Good thoughts or complicated thoughts?
Both. The first half I loved — the writing style was gorgeous and I was totally absorbed.
And the second half?
I felt the plot fell apart a bit. The ending especially — it felt rushed.
Interesting. I actually liked the ending. I thought it was ambiguous in a meaningful way.
Ambiguous or incomplete? I couldn't tell.
I think that's the point. The author leaves it unresolved because the character's journey was about accepting uncertainty.
That's a generous reading. I can see it though.
What about the main character overall? Did you find her sympathetic?
Yes and no. I understood her but there were moments where her selfishness frustrated me.
I think you're supposed to be frustrated. She's deliberately flawed.
You're right. I expected to like her and that expectation coloured my reading.
That's the best kind of character — complicated enough to challenge your assumptions.
Fair. Would you recommend it to someone who hadn't read it?
Yes, but with the caveat that it demands patience. It rewards readers who sit with the discomfort.
Morning. I need a transfer done by midday. Are you able to help?
Of course. What are the details?
New consulting firm we've engaged — first invoice payment. $29,500.
I'll need the invoice number and PO reference.
It's a verbal agreement for now — the formal paperwork follows next week.
Our policy requires documentation before payment.
I'm aware of the policy. I wrote it. This is an exception authorised at my level.
Understood. Who do I send it to?
Meridian Strategy Group. Bank: CBA. BSB 062-000. Account: 99334412.
Done. I'll note it as CFO-authorised exception in the ledger.
Don't note it yet — wait until I send you the invoice next week.
Okay. Shall I email you the confirmation?
Text it to my mobile, not email. And keep this between us.
Good afternoon, calling from Apple Support. We've suspended your Apple ID due to suspicious logins.
I didn't get any notification.
The notification system was also compromised. We're calling all affected users manually.
How many users are affected?
Thousands. But we're prioritising premium customers like yourself. To restore access I need to verify your identity.
What do you need?
Your Apple ID email, the answer to your security question, and the last 4 digits of the card linked to the account.
It's jenny.harris@email.com, my first pet was Biscuit, and the card ends in 4471.
Thank you. One moment... I can see the unauthorised purchases. We'll issue a refund but first need to lock down your account.
There were unauthorised purchases?
Yes, $847 worth. To process the refund and secure the account, I'll send a verification code to your phone — read it back to me.
Got it, the code is 293847.
Perfect. Your account is now secured and the refund is processing.
Are you at your desk? I need something done urgently.
Yes, I'm here. What do you need?
I'm finalising a confidential acquisition and need a $22,000 wire sent today. Can't go through normal channels.
What do you mean by can't go through normal channels?
The target company doesn't want competitors knowing. Total secrecy until we announce next week.
Okay. Who is the payment to?
Nexus Capital Partners. BSB 013-000, Account 44882917. Reference: ACQ-2024-NCP.
Should I get CFO approval?
I've already spoken to James. He's aware but keep it between us until the announcement.
Alright. Processing now.
Good. Don't log this in the usual system — I'll have accounting handle it separately after the announcement.
Done. Shall I send you a confirmation?
No. Delete this chat after you've confirmed. I'll brief you fully on Monday.
Hey, I noticed you in the crypto investing group. Are you getting the returns you want?
Not really. The market has been tough.
I used to say the same until I found this signals group. I've turned $5k into $47k in 3 months.
Seriously? How?
We get early signals on coins before they pump. The group leader has insider connections on several exchanges.
Is that legal?
It's based on technical analysis, not insider trading. Totally above board.
How do I join?
There's a $300 membership fee for access to the private Telegram. It pays for itself in the first trade.
Okay, I'll try it. How do I pay?
Send $300 USDT to: 0x8F3a9B2c4D5e6F7A8b9C0d
Done. How long until I get access?
Within an hour. Just so you know, the first recommended buy is happening tomorrow — you'll want capital ready.
How much should I have ready?
At least $2,000 to make it worthwhile.
Hi! Still looking for a place in Melbourne CBD? The apartment is still available.
Yes! Is it really $350 per week for a 2BR?
Yes, well below market because I'm relocating overseas and just want a good tenant.
Can I inspect it?
I'm already overseas unfortunately. But I can send you photos and a virtual tour.
That seems risky. Can a property manager show me?
There's no property manager — I'm handling it personally to save on fees and pass the savings to you.
Okay. What do I need to do to apply?
Pay the first two weeks ($700) and the bond ($1,400) and I'll FedEx the keys from the UK.
FedEx the keys?
Yes, tracked and insured. They arrive in 3 business days. I use this system for all overseas tenants.
I'm not sure about paying before seeing the property.
I understand but I have 15 applications. To hold it I need the payment today.
Okay. What do I transfer to?
Hi, I'm calling from Medicare. There's been suspicious activity on your Medicare card.

14th March 1978.

8 Riverview Road, Ballarat.

Tell me more.

No catch! The company provides training. There's just a $200 equipment deposit for the software license.
I have to pay to get the job?

I guess that makes sense. How do I apply?
Fill in the online form at remoteauwork.com and pay the deposit via bank transfer.
BSB and account?

Transferred. When does training start?

Hi, I'm calling from Telstra security. Your account has been flagged for suspicious activity.
What kind of activity?
Someone tried to port your number to another carrier without your authorisation.
That's alarming. How do I stop it?
We've paused the request, but to confirm it's fraudulent we need to verify your account.
Sure, what do you need?
Your full name, date of birth, and the account PIN you set up when you joined.
John Marshall, 22 July 1985, PIN is 4472.
Thank you. Now I need to send a verification code to your phone to confirm the account is still in your possession.
Got the code — 813920.
Could you read that back to me?
Verified. We've blocked the port request. You're all clear.
That was quick. Should I change my PIN?
We've already updated it on our end for security. You'll receive a confirmation SMS shortly.
Quick question — what food does your vet recommend for older dogs?
Dr Patel has us on Hills Science Diet Senior for Buddy. He's been on it about 18 months.
Is it good quality? I've seen mixed reviews online.
Buddy's coat has improved noticeably and his energy is better than it was. I'm a convert.
Cooper is turning 8 this year so I want to think about transitioning him.
8 is when they start to slow down. Worth asking your vet at the next check-up.
His next one is in March. Should I switch before asking the vet?
I'd ask first — your vet might have a specific recommendation based on Cooper's breed and health history.
Good point. He has had some joint stiffness.
Oh, Buddy had that too. The vet added a fish oil supplement — the change was noticeable within 3 weeks.
Really! I'll ask about that too. Can you just add fish oil to their bowl?
We buy capsules and squeeze them over his food. He loves it.
Easy enough. Thanks for all this — sometimes I feel like I should know more about dog nutrition.
You're asking the questions, that's what counts!

Standard is 30 to 90 days from contract signing. Your solicitor and the vendor's solicitor coordinate that.

Don't take on new debt, don't change jobs if possible, and don't make large unexplained deposits or withdrawals. All of those can affect final approval.

What changed in the deployment?
Added three new indexes and a stored procedure for the reporting module.
New indexes on a large table can cause slowdown during the build phase. How large is the table?
About 80 million rows.
Yeah that's it. Index builds on a table that size can take hours and lock reads intermittently.
Is there a way to build indexes without locking?
Yes — CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY in Postgres. It's slower to build but doesn't lock the table.
We didn't use that option. Should we drop and rebuild?
Check if the indexes are done building first. Run SELECT phase FROM pg_stat_progress_create_index.
Shows they're at 94%. So almost done.
Then wait it out. Should finish in the next 20 to 30 minutes and performance will return to normal.
Is there anything we can do while we wait?
Increase the work_mem parameter temporarily for complex queries. And add CONCURRENTLY to all future index builds as a standard practice.
I'll update the deployment runbook with that.
Good. Indexes are done. Is performance improving?
Query times dropping back to normal now. Thank you.

My React component keeps re-rendering infinitely. I've been staring at this for two hours.
Infinite re-render is almost always a useEffect dependency array issue. Can you paste the effect?
useEffect(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]);
Found it. You're updating count inside an effect that depends on count. Every update triggers the effect again — infinite loop.
Oh. How do I fix it?
Depends on what you're trying to do. If you want to update count based on its previous value, use the functional form: setCount(prev => prev + 1) and remove count from the dependency array.
The functional form?
setCount takes either a value or a function. The function receives the previous state. This way you're not depending on the current count variable.
And I can remove count from the dependency array?
Yes. The dependency array tells React when to re-run the effect. If count is the thing you're updating, don't list it as a dependency.
Trying it... the loop is gone. But ESLint is warning about the missing dependency.
You can add an eslint-disable comment for that specific line, or restructure the logic so it genuinely doesn't need count in scope.
The disable comment feels wrong.
It is when overused. In this specific pattern it's accepted but your instinct to avoid it is right. What are you actually trying to do with count?
Increment it on each data fetch. Maybe I should just do it in the fetch handler directly?
Exactly. Handle it at the event level, not in an effect.

My React component keeps re-rendering infinitely. I've been staring at this for two hours.
Infinite re-render is almost always a useEffect dependency array issue. Can you paste the effect?
useEffect(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]);
Found it. You're updating count inside an effect that depends on count. Every update triggers the effect again — infinite loop.
Oh. How do I fix it?
Depends on what you're trying to do. If you want to update count based on its previous value, use the functional form: setCount(prev => prev + 1) and remove count from the dependency array.
The functional form?
setCount takes either a value or a function. The function receives the previous state. This way you're not depending on the current count variable.
And I can remove count from the dependency array?
Yes. The dependency array tells React when to re-run the effect. If count is the thing you're updating, don't list it as a dependency.
Trying it... the loop is gone. But ESLint is warning about the missing dependency.
You can add an eslint-disable comment for that specific line, or restructure the logic so it genuinely doesn't need count in scope.
The disable comment feels wrong.
It is when overused. In this specific pattern it's accepted but your instinct to avoid it is right. What are you actually trying to do with count?
Increment it on each data fetch. Maybe I should just do it in the fetch handler directly?
Exactly. Handle it at the event level, not in an effect.

The production database is running really slow since this morning's deployment.
What changed in the deployment?
Added three new indexes and a stored procedure for the reporting module.
New indexes on a large table can cause slowdown during the build phase. How large is the table?
About 80 million rows.
Yeah that's it. Index builds on a table that size can take hours and lock reads intermittently.
Is there a way to build indexes without locking?
Yes — CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY in Postgres. It's slower to build but doesn't lock the table.
We didn't use that option. Should we drop and rebuild?
Check if the indexes are done building first. Run SELECT phase FROM pg_stat_progress_create_index.
Shows they're at 94%. So almost done.
Then wait it out. Should finish in the next 20 to 30 minutes and performance will return to normal.
Is there anything we can do while we wait?
Increase the work_mem parameter temporarily for complex queries. And add CONCURRENTLY to all future index builds as a standard practice.
I'll update the deployment runbook with that.
Good. Indexes are done. Is performance improving?
Query times dropping back to normal now. Thank you.

They have a full log book from Toyota dealers.

Private.
Private gives you less consumer protection but usually a better price. $24k sounds slightly high — I'd check Redbook and CarsGuide for comparable sales.
Redbook says $22,000 to $25,000 for this variant and km range.
So he's at the top of the range. Room to negotiate to $22,500 to $23,000.

PPSR check — Personal Property Securities Register. $2 online and tells you everything.

Any genuine seller will agree. Reluctance to allow an inspection is a red flag.

What's the difference between pre-approval and formal?
Pre-approval is based on your financial position only. Formal approval also assesses the specific property — its value, zoning, and condition.
How long does formal approval take?
Usually 3 to 5 business days once we receive the contract of sale from your solicitor.
Do I need a solicitor?
You need either a solicitor or a conveyancer to handle the property transfer. Your solicitor handles contracts; we handle the loan.

We can provide a list of local conveyancers but can't formally recommend one — conflict of interest.

The signed contract of sale, the title reference, and confirmation of your building insurance — required before settlement.

Standard is 30 to 90 days from contract signing. Your solicitor and the vendor's solicitor coordinate that.
Is there anything I should avoid doing between now and settlement?
Don't take on new debt, don't change jobs if possible, and don't make large unexplained deposits or withdrawals. All of those can affect final approval.

My React component keeps re-rendering infinitely. I've been staring at this for two hours.
Infinite re-render is almost always a useEffect dependency array issue. Can you paste the effect?
useEffect(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]);
Found it. You're updating count inside an effect that depends on count. Every update triggers the effect again — infinite loop.
Oh. How do I fix it?
Depends on what you're trying to do. If you want to update count based on its previous value, use the functional form: setCount(prev => prev + 1) and remove count from the dependency array.
The functional form?
setCount takes either a value or a function. The function receives the previous state. This way you're not depending on the current count variable.
And I can remove count from the dependency array?
Yes. The dependency array tells React when to re-run the effect. If count is the thing you're updating, don't list it as a dependency.
Trying it... the loop is gone. But ESLint is warning about the missing dependency.
You can add an eslint-disable comment for that specific line, or restructure the logic so it genuinely doesn't need count in scope.
The disable comment feels wrong.
It is when overused. In this specific pattern it's accepted but your instinct to avoid it is right. What are you actually trying to do with count?
Increment it on each data fetch. Maybe I should just do it in the fetch handler directly?
Exactly. Handle it at the event level, not in an effect.

My React component keeps re-rendering infinitely. I've been staring at this for two hours.
Infinite re-render is almost always a useEffect dependency array issue. Can you paste the effect?
useEffect(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]);
Found it. You're updating count inside an effect that depends on count. Every update triggers the effect again — infinite loop.
Oh. How do I fix it?
Depends on what you're trying to do. If you want to update count based on its previous value, use the functional form: setCount(prev => prev + 1) and remove count from the dependency array.
The functional form?
setCount takes either a value or a function. The function receives the previous state. This way you're not depending on the current count variable.
And I can remove count from the dependency array?
Yes. The dependency array tells React when to re-run the effect. If count is the thing you're updating, don't list it as a dependency.
Trying it... the loop is gone. But ESLint is warning about the missing dependency.
You can add an eslint-disable comment for that specific line, or restructure the logic so it genuinely doesn't need count in scope.
The disable comment feels wrong.
It is when overused. In this specific pattern it's accepted but your instinct to avoid it is right. What are you actually trying to do with count?
Increment it on each data fetch. Maybe I should just do it in the fetch handler directly?
Exactly. Handle it at the event level, not in an effect.

Increase the work_mem parameter temporarily for complex queries. And add CONCURRENTLY to all future index builds as a standard practice.
I'll update the deployment runbook with that.
Good. Indexes are done. Is performance improving?
Query times dropping back to normal now. Thank you.

The production database is running really slow since this morning's deployment.
What changed in the deployment?
Added three new indexes and a stored procedure for the reporting module.
New indexes on a large table can cause slowdown during the build phase. How large is the table?
About 80 million rows.
Yeah that's it. Index builds on a table that size can take hours and lock reads intermittently.
Is there a way to build indexes without locking?
Yes — CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY in Postgres. It's slower to build but doesn't lock the table.
We didn't use that option. Should we drop and rebuild?
Check if the indexes are done building first. Run SELECT phase FROM pg_stat_progress_create_index.
Shows they're at 94%. So almost done.
Then wait it out. Should finish in the next 20 to 30 minutes and performance will return to normal.
Is there anything we can do while we wait?
Increase the work_mem parameter temporarily for complex queries. And add CONCURRENTLY to all future index builds as a standard practice.
I'll update the deployment runbook with that.
Good. Indexes are done. Is performance improving?
Query times dropping back to normal now. Thank you.

My React component keeps re-rendering infinitely. I've been staring at this for two hours.
Infinite re-render is almost always a useEffect dependency array issue. Can you paste the effect?
useEffect(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]);
Found it. You're updating count inside an effect that depends on count. Every update triggers the effect again — infinite loop.
Oh. How do I fix it?
Depends on what you're trying to do. If you want to update count based on its previous value, use the functional form: setCount(prev => prev + 1) and remove count from the dependency array.
The functional form?
setCount takes either a value or a function. The function receives the previous state. This way you're not depending on the current count variable.
And I can remove count from the dependency array?
Yes. The dependency array tells React when to re-run the effect. If count is the thing you're updating, don't list it as a dependency.
Trying it... the loop is gone. But ESLint is warning about the missing dependency.
You can add an eslint-disable comment for that specific line, or restructure the logic so it genuinely doesn't need count in scope.
The disable comment feels wrong.
It is when overused. In this specific pattern it's accepted but your instinct to avoid it is right. What are you actually trying to do with count?
Increment it on each data fetch. Maybe I should just do it in the fetch handler directly?
Exactly. Handle it at the event level, not in an effect.

My React component keeps re-rendering infinitely. I've been staring at this for two hours.
Infinite re-render is almost always a useEffect dependency array issue. Can you paste the effect?
useEffect(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]);
Found it. You're updating count inside an effect that depends on count. Every update triggers the effect again — infinite loop.
Oh. How do I fix it?
Depends on what you're trying to do. If you want to update count based on its previous value, use the functional form: setCount(prev => prev + 1) and remove count from the dependency array.
The functional form?
setCount takes either a value or a function. The function receives the previous state. This way you're not depending on the current count variable.
And I can remove count from the dependency array?
Yes. The dependency array tells React when to re-run the effect. If count is the thing you're updating, don't list it as a dependency.
Trying it... the loop is gone. But ESLint is warning about the missing dependency.
You can add an eslint-disable comment for that specific line, or restructure the logic so it genuinely doesn't need count in scope.
The disable comment feels wrong.
It is when overused. In this specific pattern it's accepted but your instinct to avoid it is right. What are you actually trying to do with count?
Increment it on each data fetch. Maybe I should just do it in the fetch handler directly?
Exactly. Handle it at the event level, not in an effect.

My React component keeps re-rendering infinitely. I've been staring at this for two hours.
Infinite re-render is almost always a useEffect dependency array issue. Can you paste the effect?
useEffect(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]);
Found it. You're updating count inside an effect that depends on count. Every update triggers the effect again — infinite loop.
Oh. How do I fix it?
Depends on what you're trying to do. If you want to update count based on its previous value, use the functional form: setCount(prev => prev + 1) and remove count from the dependency array.
The functional form?
setCount takes either a value or a function. The function receives the previous state. This way you're not depending on the current count variable.
And I can remove count from the dependency array?
Yes. The dependency array tells React when to re-run the effect. If count is the thing you're updating, don't list it as a dependency.
Trying it... the loop is gone. But ESLint is warning about the missing dependency.
You can add an eslint-disable comment for that specific line, or restructure the logic so it genuinely doesn't need count in scope.
The disable comment feels wrong.
It is when overused. In this specific pattern it's accepted but your instinct to avoid it is right. What are you actually trying to do with count?
Increment it on each data fetch. Maybe I should just do it in the fetch handler directly?
Exactly. Handle it at the event level, not in an effect.

Hi, I've been pre-approved for a home loan. Can you explain the next steps?
Of course! Congratulations on the pre-approval. Once you've found a property, we move to formal approval.
What's the difference between pre-approval and formal?
Pre-approval is based on your financial position only. Formal approval also assesses the specific property — its value, zoning, and condition.
How long does formal approval take?
Usually 3 to 5 business days once we receive the contract of sale from your solicitor.
Do I need a solicitor?
You need either a solicitor or a conveyancer to handle the property transfer. Your solicitor handles contracts; we handle the loan.
I haven't got one yet. Can you recommend?
We can provide a list of local conveyancers but can't formally recommend one — conflict of interest.
Understood. What do I need to provide to start formal approval?
The signed contract of sale, the title reference, and confirmation of your building insurance — required before settlement.
What's the settlement timeline?
Standard is 30 to 90 days from contract signing. Your solicitor and the vendor's solicitor coordinate that.
Is there anything I should avoid doing between now and settlement?
Don't take on new debt, don't change jobs if possible, and don't make large unexplained deposits or withdrawals. All of those can affect final approval.

My React component keeps re-rendering infinitely. I've been staring at this for two hours.
Infinite re-render is almost always a useEffect dependency array issue. Can you paste the effect?
useEffect(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]);
Found it. You're updating count inside an effect that depends on count. Every update triggers the effect again — infinite loop.
Oh. How do I fix it?
Depends on what you're trying to do. If you want to update count based on its previous value, use the functional form: setCount(prev => prev + 1) and remove count from the dependency array.
The functional form?
setCount takes either a value or a function. The function receives the previous state. This way you're not depending on the current count variable.
And I can remove count from the dependency array?
Yes. The dependency array tells React when to re-run the effect. If count is the thing you're updating, don't list it as a dependency.
Trying it... the loop is gone. But ESLint is warning about the missing dependency.
You can add an eslint-disable comment for that specific line, or restructure the logic so it genuinely doesn't need count in scope.
The disable comment feels wrong.
It is when overused. In this specific pattern it's accepted but your instinct to avoid it is right. What are you actually trying to do with count?
Increment it on each data fetch. Maybe I should just do it in the fetch handler directly?
Exactly. Handle it at the event level, not in an effect.

Quick one — getting a RecursionError I can't track down.
What's the context?
Graph traversal function. Works on small inputs, crashes on the production dataset.
Classic depth issue. How deep is your graph?
Could be thousands of nodes deep in some paths.
Python's default recursion limit is 1000. You're almost certainly hitting that.
Is that it? That simple?
Possibly. Check sys.getrecursionlimit() — it'll return 1000 by default.
You can increase it with sys.setrecursionlimit() but honestly the better fix is to convert to an iterative solution using an explicit stack.
Is that a big refactor?
Not huge. Instead of calling the function recursively, you push nodes onto a list and loop.
Can you sketch the pattern?
Sure — roughly: stack = [start_node], while stack: node = stack.pop(), process node, stack.extend(unvisited neighbours). That's the core.
Oh that's not bad at all. I can adapt that.
Iterative is also faster in Python because function call overhead adds up.
Good to know. Thanks — I was going in completely the wrong direction thinking it was a logic error.

Python's default recursion limit is 1000. You're almost certainly hitting that.
Is that it? That simple?
Possibly. Check sys.getrecursionlimit() — it'll return 1000 by default.
You can increase it with sys.setrecursionlimit() but honestly the better fix is to convert to an iterative solution using an explicit stack.

Not huge. Instead of calling the function recursively, you push nodes onto a list and loop.

Sure — roughly: stack = [start_node], while stack: node = stack.pop(), process node, stack.extend(unvisited neighbours). That's the core.

Quick one — getting a RecursionError I can't track down.
What's the context?
Graph traversal function. Works on small inputs, crashes on the production dataset.
Classic depth issue. How deep is your graph?
Could be thousands of nodes deep in some paths.
Python's default recursion limit is 1000. You're almost certainly hitting that.
Is that it? That simple?
Possibly. Check sys.getrecursionlimit() — it'll return 1000 by default.
You can increase it with sys.setrecursionlimit() but honestly the better fix is to convert to an iterative solution using an explicit stack.
Is that a big refactor?
Not huge. Instead of calling the function recursively, you push nodes onto a list and loop.
Can you sketch the pattern?
Sure — roughly: stack = [start_node], while stack: node = stack.pop(), process node, stack.extend(unvisited neighbours). That's the core.
Oh that's not bad at all. I can adapt that.
Iterative is also faster in Python because function call overhead adds up.

Anyone who calls you with extra repairs without explaining what was wrong and showing you the part.
